title: Goroutines, Channels, Select, Ownership, and Pipelines · Go Semantics Case: Select Fairness Timeouts
summary: A report pipeline must process bounded work concurrently, preserve result ownership, close channels correctly, and terminate every goroutine. Planned artifact: Add and verify the go select fairness timeouts increment in a bounded concurrent report pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use select, nil-channel disabling, default, timers, and explicit deadlines without assuming deterministic ready-case choice.
Distinguish open, closed, empty, nil, and buffered channel states and interpret two-result receives and range termination.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.code:
package main

import "fmt"

func main() {
	results := make(chan int, 1)
	go func() { results <- 5 * 5; close(results) }()
	value, ok := <-results
	fmt.Println(value, ok)
}
content.caption: Runnable Go value, failure, or concurrency contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
